channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hurricane Milton: Protect your home from looting with these 4 steps
item.description: Emergency preparedness expert Geoff Fahringer explains how to make your home unattractive to would-be looters as Hurricane Milton approaches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Climate change boosted Helene's deadly rain and wind and scientists say same is likely for Milton
item.description: Scientists say human-caused climate change boosted the rainfall of deadly Hurricane Helene by about 10% and intensified its winds by about 11%.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Members of LDS Church, Utahns help those affected by Helene, prepare for Milton
item.description: Members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints along with a Davis County business and the American Red Cross are helping many pick up the pieces after Hurricane Helene ravaged the southeast.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, 96-year-old widow of Robert F. Kennedy, suffers stroke, family asks for prayers
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, mother to eleven children, wife to the late Sen. Robert F. Kennedy, and long-time philanthropist, has suffered from a stroke, her family announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Washington state woman's yard invaded by more than 100 raccoons after 3 decades of feeding them
item.description: A Washington state woman had her yard invaded by more than 100 raccoons last week. She said she's been feeding about a dozen of them for about 38 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VIDEO: Hurricane hunters take turbulent trip through Milton
item.description: Researchers on a hurricane hunter aircraft had a bumpy ride as they flew into Hurricane Milton on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia university leaders ask NCAA to ban transgender women from sports
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The regents who govern Georgia's 26 public universities and colleges voted on Tuesday to ask the NCAA and another college athletic federation to ban transgender women from participating in women's sports. The unanimous vote came after Georgia Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How will Hurricane Milton stack up against other major recent storms?
item.description: As a ferocious Hurricane Milton charges its way across the Gulf Coast with Tampa and St. Petersburg in its sights, thousands flee the area for safer ground, leaving behind debris from Hurricane Helene's destruction less that two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton as it churns toward Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: Hurricane Milton strengthened as it churned through the Gulf of Mexico toward Florida on Tuesday and could wallop one of the state's major population centers with huge storm surges, lashing rain and destructive winds just two weeks after deadly Hurricane Helene swamped the coastline.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden sets 10-year deadline for US cities to replace lead pipes nationwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A decade after the Flint, Michigan, water crisis raised alarms about the continuing dangers of lead in tap water, President Joe Biden on Tuesday set a 10-year deadline for cities across the nation to replace their lead pipes, finalizing an aggressive approach aimed at ensuring that
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How do I know if my mail-in vote was counted?
item.description: There are varying ways in which someone can qualify for mail voting; absentee voting (excuse or no-excuse) and standardized mail voting.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court to decide whether Oklahoma must execute death row inmate
item.description: Richard Glossip, an Oklahoma death row inmate, has been scheduled for execution nine times — and each time, he has avoided that fate following legal twists and turns. Convicted for the 1997 killing of his former boss, Glossip has maintained his innocence for nearly three decades.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Airline apologizes after playing explicit movie on every screen
item.description: Australia's Qantas Airways is apologizing after showing a movie that contains scenes of nudity, graphic language and sexting to the passengers aboard a recent flight from Sydney to Haneda, Japan.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Floridians prepare for the worst as Hurricane Milton quickly approaches
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor took to television with a dire warning for those staying behind in evacuation zones ahead of Hurricane Milton's landfall.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Milton may be October surprise in presidential battle
item.description: Strategists in both parties are looking back to Hurricane Sandy, which slammed into the mid-Atlantic states shortly before Election Day 2012.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Bizarre laws in Washington, such as being charged with reckless driving if hugging while behind the wheel
item.description: Among Washington's strangest laws include penalties for using X-rays as shoe-fitting devices as well as trouble for intimidating another with a laser.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fearful residents flee Tampa Bay region as Hurricane Milton takes aim at Florida coast
item.description: Fearful Florida residents streamed out of the Tampa Bay region Tuesday ahead of what could be a once-in-a-century direct hit from Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The mayor of a state capital in Mexico is killed less than 1 week after he took office
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Relatives on Monday buried the mayor of a state capital in southern Mexico who was killed just one week after he took office. Photos of the crime scene shared on social media showed Mayor Alejandro Arcos’ severed head had been left on the roof of a pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amid cyberattack, nation's largest water utility pauses billing
item.description: American Water provides services to more than 14 million people across 14 states and on 18 military installations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal court reviews civil rights lawsuit alleging environmental racism in a Louisiana parish
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A federal appellate court is reviewing a civil rights lawsuit alleging a south Louisiana parish engaged in racist land-use policies to place polluting industries in majority-Black communities. The Fifth U.S.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As Milton takes aim at Florida, why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: The last time the eye of a major storm like Hurricane Milton struck Tampa Bay, in 1921, the city was a sleepy backwater of a few hundred thousand people.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Costco gold bars are selling out as prices for the precious metal break records: survey
item.description: Move over, gas and hot dogs. Costco shoppers are snatching up the store's 1-ounce gold bars despite the market's lofty price for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Chicago mayor names new school board after entire panel resigns amid a fight over district control
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson named members of a new school board on Monday, batting away criticism of the move days after all seven members resigned amid an escalating fight over control of the public schools in the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert in NC
item.description: "Concert for Carolina," featuring North Carolina natives James Taylor, Luke Combs and Eric Church, will be held at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte with all proceeds going to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, organizers announced.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, ABC 4

item.title: Live Wobble Tracker: Map shows real-time movements of Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton, which reached Category 5 strength Monday, is heading right for Florida's west coast, with a potential landfall near or at Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear Pennsylvania legislators' challenge to executive actions expanding voting access
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday it won’t take up a case to restore a lawsuit by Pennsylvania Republicans that challenged executive actions expanding voting access on the basis that only state legislatures can regulate federal elections.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: LeBron and son Bronny James play together for the first time in a preseason game for the Lakers
item.description: PALM DESERT, Calif. (AP) — LeBron James grew up without a father, and the top scorer in NBA history has often said he savors every moment and milestone he can experience with his own three children.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns scores 10 points in abbreviated preseason debut with New York Knicks
item.description: Jalen Brunson thought twice about going 1-on-2 in transition, instead dribbled back outside and flipped the ball behind him to Karl-Anthony Towns, who made a 3-pointer with a defender in his face.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ohtani homers in playoff debut and the Dodgers rally to beat the Padres 7-5 in NLDS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani hit a three-run homer in his postseason debut, Teoscar Hernández’s two-run single gave Los Angeles its first lead in a playoff game in two years, and the Dodgers beat the San Diego Padres 7-5 in their NL Division Series opener Saturday.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Supreme Court opens its new term with election disputes in the air but not yet on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Transgender rights, the regulation of “ghost guns” and the death penalty highlight the Supreme Court's election-season term that begins Monday, with the prospect of the court's intervention in voting disputes lurking in the background.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Idaho state senator tells Native American candidate 'go back where you came from' in forum
item.description: KENDRICK, Idaho (AP) — Tensions rose during a bipartisan forum this week after an audience question about discrimination reportedly led an Idaho state senator to angrily tell a Native American candidate to “go back where you came from.” Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4
